REPLY BRIEF IN SUPPORT OF DEMURRER TO THE COMPALINT FILED BY DEFENDANT FOLLANSBEE, JULIA

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JULIA FOLLANSBEE'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO QUASH IS DENIED. DEFENDANT REACHED OUT TO CALIFORNIA TO TRANSACT BUSINESS. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM DIRECTLY ARISES FROM THAT CONTACT. MOTION TO DISMISS ON GROUNDS OF FORUM NON CONVENIENS IS DENIED. ALTHOUGH OREGON IS LIKELY A SUITABLE FORUM, INSUFFICIENT EVIDENCE WHETHER THE BALANCE OF PUBLIC AND PRIVATE INTERESTS SHOWS THAT IT WOULD BE MORE JUST TO FILE THE CASE ELSEWHERE. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW THAT CALIFORNIA IS A SERIOUSLY INCONVENIENT FORUM. THE COURT ADDS: DISMISSAL IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE. DEFENSE COUNSEL WILL PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JULIA FOLLANSBEE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PLEAD DAMAGE OR AN ACTIONABLE MISREPRESENTATION. GENERALLY AN ACTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION MUST BE MADE AS TO PAST OR EXISTING FACTS. PREDICTIONS AS TO FUTURE EVENTS, OR STATEMENTS AS TO FUTURE ACTION BY SOME THIRD PARTY, ARE DEEMED OPINIONS, AND ARE NOT ACTIONABLE FRAUD. BORBA VS. THOMAS (1977) 70 CAL.APP. 3RD 144. DEFENSE COUNSEL WILL PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
MINI-MINUTES FOR MAY-21-2009 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-10-2009 Off Calendar

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar May-21-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JULIA FOLLANSBEE'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Appearances: Mark L. Webb, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 621-4500, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Nelson E. Brestoff, Esq. of Nelson E. Brestoff Professional Law, (818) 787-4077, Appearing thru CourtCall, for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted and modified its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to quash is denied. Defendant reached out to California to transact business. Plaintiff's claim directly arises from that contact. Motion to dismiss on grounds of forum non conveniens is denied. Although Oregon is likely a suitable forum, insufficient evidence whether the balance of public and private interests shows that it would be more just to file the case elsewhere. Defendant fails to show that California is a seriously inconvenient forum. The Court adds: Dismissal is without prejudice. Defense Counsel will prepare Order. (302/CWW/PB)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-21-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JULIA FOLLANSBEE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Appearances: Mark L. Webb, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 621-4500, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Nelson E. Brestoff, Esq. of Nelson E. Brestoff Professional Law, (818) 787-4077, Appearing thru CourtCall, for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to plead damage or an actionable misrepresentation. Generally an actional misrepresentation must be made as to past or existing facts. Predictions as to future events, or statements as to future action by some third party, are deemed opinions, and are not actionable fraud. Borba vs. Thomas (1977) 70 Cal.App. 3rd 144. Defense counsel will prepare Order. (302/CWW/PB)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
